content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1160 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

September 23, 1976


Leonard Panish
Registrar-Recorder
County of Los Angeles
Department of Registrar-Recorder
808 North Spring
Los Angeles, CA  90012

Re: Opinion Request
    No. 76-083

Dear Mr. Panish:

     Thank you for your letter of September 2, 1976, requesting an opinion with respect to the reporting obligations for central committee candidates pursuant to Government Code Section 84200.5 of the Political Reform Act. Since your letter raises no substantial question involving an interpretation of the Political Reform Act, no formal opinion will be issued. However, I hope the following informal comments will be helpful.

     Section 84200.5 provides that a candidate for county central committee shall file a single campaign statement within 38 days following the election and shall not be required to file any other campaign statements "unless he has received contributions for that election in which case he shall observe the requirements of Section 84200." In your letter, you indicate that the filer in question received "minor post election contributions." We think that such post election contributions are "contributions for that election" within the meaning of Section 84200.5 so long as they are received in connection with the election for which the report is filed. Accordingly, we conclude that the filer has not acted in violation of the Political Reform Act by filing his campaign statement within 65 days after the election in accordance with the provisions of Section 84200.

     You also may be interested to know that the Legislature recently passed AB 1362 which, if signed by the Governor, will permit candidates for county central committee to file

[6]

Leonard Panish
September 23, 1976
Page Two


post election campaign statements within 65 days following
the election in all instances.

     If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion
request, you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
1o321. If you have any further questions, please contact Ted
Prin, an attorney with the Commission's Legal Division.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Michael Bennett
                                        Executive Director

MB:TP:plh

Request No. _________
Date Received 9-8-76
Response Due 9-22-76

COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES
DEPARTMENT OF REGISTRAR—RECORD
508 NORTH SPRING, LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA, 90012

LEONARD PANISH
REGISTRAR-RECORDER

CHARLES L. CAMPBELL, JR.
CHIEF DEPUTY

September 2, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

REQUEST FOR OPINION

REFERENCE: Government Code Sections 84200, 84200.5

Gentlemen and Mesdames:

The following circumstances prompt this request for an opinion.

Section 84200.5 permits a candidate for County Central Committee who has received no contributions to file a single campaign statement within 38 days following the election.

A situation has arisen wherein a candidate did not receive contributions prior to the election. Accordingly, three days after the 38 day filing deadline (July 16) he was mailed a specific notice of the filing requirement. His response was not received within five days and a second notice was sent. On August 3 he submitted a campaign statement with the comment: "I had no previous election receipts, but did have minor post-election contributions under $200 total. Thus I am filing on the normal post-election filing schedule."

(Our Department had been advised in advance that a deliberate test of the 38 day filing provision would be made by a candidate who would file just prior to the 65 day deadline.)

The opinion I am requesting is:

May a candidate for County Central Committee who has not filed pre-election statements file a post-election statement using the deadline date specified in section 84200 of 65 days after the election, rather than 38 days as specified in section 84200.5?

Thank you for your consideration.

Very truly yours,

Leonard Panish

LEONARD PANISH
Registrar-Recorder

sr

sections.question: May a Candidate for County Central Committee who has not filed pre-election statements file a posteelection statement using the deadline date specified in section 84200 of 65 days after the election, rather than 35 days as specified in section 84200.5?
sections.conclusion: We think that such post election contributions are "contributions for that election" within the meaning of Section 84200.5 so long as they are received in connection with the election for which the report is filed. Accordingly, we conclude that the filer has not acted in violation of the Political Reform Act by filing his campaign statement within 65 days after the election in accordance with the provisions of Section 84200.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a county central committee candidate who received no pre-election contributions but received minor post-election contributions may file a campaign statement within 65 days (rather than 35 days) after the election under Government Code Section 84200.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Post-election contributions received in connection with the election constitute 'contributions for that election' under Section 84200.5, requiring the candidate to follow Section 84200's 65-day filing deadline rather than the 35-day deadline for candidates with no contributions. The candidate did not violate the Political Reform Act by filing within 65 days.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated September 2, 1975) and the FPPC response letter (dated September 23, 1976, though year appears to be 1975 based on content). Image quality is poor with some OCR errors. The response indicates no formal opinion will be issued as no substantial question of interpretation exists, but informal advice is provided. Reference number 76-083 appears to be assigned by FPPC.

QUESTION: May a Candidate for County Central Committee who has not filed pre-election statements file a posteelection statement using the deadline date specified in section 84200 of 65 days after the election, rather than 35 days as specified in section 84200.5?

CONCLUSION: We think that such post election contributions are "contributions for that election" within the meaning of Section 84200.5 so long as they are received in connection with the election for which the report is filed. Accordingly, we conclude that the filer has not acted in violation of the Political Reform Act by filing his campaign statement within 65 days after the election in accordance with the provisions of Section 84200.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a request from the Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder regarding reporting obligations for a county central committee candidate who received minor post-election contributions. The Commission advises that post-election contributions trigger full campaign reporting requirements under Section 84200, allowing a 65-day filing deadline.
